- image node:20 or python:3.12
- script commands

Compare to GitHub Actions in 3 bullets.

prompts.label: Environment secrets in CI
prompts.text:
How to store API keys in GitHub Actions secrets and use in workflow.

- Never print secrets in logs
- Rotation reminder

Example env: API_KEY: ${{ secrets.API_KEY }}

prompts.label: Matrix build Node versions
prompts.text:
strategy.matrix node-version [18, 20] run tests on both.

- Why matrix helps

Yaml snippet.

prompts.label: Manual approval production
prompts.text:
GitHub environments with required reviewers before prod deploy.

- When teams use manual gate

Process description.

prompts.label: CI failure debug checklist
prompts.text:
Checklist when tests pass locally but fail in CI.

- env vars, node version, timezone, file paths case sensitivity
- Re-run with debug logging

7 bullets.

prompts.label: Reusable GitHub workflows
prompts.text:
Extract a shared Node build workflow that other workflows call with workflow_call.
Explain how to pass inputs and inherit secrets without leaking them in logs.
Recommend pinning third-party actions to full commit SHAs.
Mention concurrency groups that cancel outdated runs when a new push arrives.
Note one pitfall: reusable workflows and permissions defaults.

prompts.label: OIDC to cloud deploy roles
prompts.text:
Replace static cloud access keys in CI with OIDC trust to a short-lived role.
Walk through the high-level trust relationship (identity provider, audience, subject claims).
Explain why temporary credentials reduce breach blast radius.
List two things that often break the first time (wrong trust policy, wrong audience).
Mention that each cloud vendor names the pieces differently.

prompts.label: actions/cache for dependencies
prompts.text:
Set up actions/cache for node_modules keyed by a hash of the lockfile.
Explain warm cache hits vs cold misses in plain language.
Describe when you should also cache global tool folders.
Warn about caching secrets or huge artifacts blindly.
Add a reminder to measure job durations before and after.

prompts.label: Downstream pipeline triggers
prompts.text:
Describe chaining a deployment repo pipeline after a build pipeline publishes artifacts.
Explain passing version strings and masked secrets safely.
Compare this approach to a monorepo for small teams.
Mention notifying humans when a chained deploy fails.
Keep the example conceptual without vendor-locking every YAML keyword.

prompts.label: Blue-green deployments vocabulary
prompts.text:
Explain blue-green as two stacks where traffic switches from old to new.
Tie this to DB migration strategies that must stay forward compatible.
Mention rollback as switching traffic back when health checks fail.
Contrast with recreate strategies that incur downtime.
Advise rehearsing releases in staging with the same playbook.

prompts.label: Canary releases progressively
prompts.text:
Explain shifting a small percentage of traffic to a new version while watching error rates and latency.
List signals you would watch before increasing traffic (5xx spikes, saturation).
Discuss feature flags versus canaries and when each helps.
Warn that canaries without good observability are mostly theater.
Avoid recommending a specific service mesh unless asked.

prompts.label: Artifact retention hygiene
prompts.text:
Recommend retention windows for CI artifacts based on usefulness vs storage cost.
Explain separating short-lived build artifacts from long-term compliance archives.
Warn about accidental secret uploads in zipped artifacts.
Suggest naming immutable artifacts with versions.
Mention egress costs if teams download huge artifacts often.

prompts.label: Branch protection basics
prompts.text:
Summarize branch protection: required status checks, required reviews, disallow force-push.
Explain why admins should still follow the rules on shared branches.
Mention CODEOWNERS for sensitive areas.
Relate squash merge policies to readable history debates.
Encourage humane review norms, not bureaucratic misery.

prompts.label: Post-deploy synthetic smoke checks
prompts.text:
Add a CI/CD step after deploy that hits critical HTTPS endpoints with timeouts.
Compare synthetic checks versus only reading logs.
Discuss tying failures to automated rollback cautiously.
Explain reducing alert noise by testing stable public routes.
Recommend keeping checks fast and focused.

prompts.label: Self-hosted runners trade-offs
prompts.text:
Explain why teams use self-hosted runners (private network access, large caches).
List responsibilities: patching OS images, rotating tokens, isolation between jobs.
Compare ephemeral runners vs long-lived VMs from a security perspective.
Warn about sharing one runner machine across unrelated teams without isolation.
Recommend cloud-hosted runners when setup burden outweighs gains.
